title: SpecValue
description: A single electrical or physical specification value with provenance. Every numeric datasheet fact is stored as an array of SpecValue entries (a field with one value still uses a one-element array).

min.description: Minimum value as published in the datasheet. null when not specified.

typ.description: Typical value. null when not specified.

max.description: Maximum value. null when not specified.

unit.description: Canonical SI unit. Extraction converts datasheet units (µA, mV, kΩ, kHz, µF, nC, etc.) before caching — consumers never parse unit prefixes. Compound units K/W and °C/W are allowed for thermal resistance (θJA, θJC, ψJT). C (coulombs) for gate charge. V/s for slew rate (store 0.6V/µs as 6e5 V/s). dB for CMRR/PSRR/open-loop gain. null for dimensionless quantities (e.g. hFE current gain, power factor).

condition.description: Test conditions under which the value holds (e.g. 'VIN=5V, IL=100mA, TJ=25°C'). Free-form string.

notes.description: Additional notes, including the original symbolic form if a symbolic expression was resolved at extraction (e.g. 'VOH = VCC − 0.4V resolved with VCC=3.3V').

page.description: PDF page number (1-based) where the fact was found.

section.description: Datasheet section label (e.g. '6.4 Electrical Characteristics') when available.

confidence.description: Extractor confidence in this fact. Pivots consumer trust gating.

method.description: How the value was captured. 'table' = direct from a parameter table. 'prose' = pulled from narrative text. 'curve' = read off a graph (lower confidence). 'calculated' = resolved from a symbolic expression. 'derived' = inferred from other datasheet facts.
